summary.intent: Implement the CRM web UI tab (CHA-19) as a headless autonomous agent, creating 4 new JS files and modifying 5 existing files to expose the already-complete CRM backend through a Preact SPA page using WebSocket RPC.
summary.outcome: All 9 files were created/modified, Rust compiled clean, biome linting passed after fixing barrel-file and unused-parameter warnings, nightly fmt applied, and the implementation was ready for cargo test + PR creation.

repo: All pages use WebSocket RPC via sendRpc() — zero pages use REST for CRUD. The engineering review corrected a CEO review that incorrectly recommended ~15 new REST handlers.
Feature gating uses cfg!(feature = "crm") in Rust and gon.get("crm_enabled") in JS, matching the graphql_enabled/voice_enabled pattern.
Top-level routes use registerPrefix (not registerPage) to handle both /crm and /crm/:contactId via a param argument.
NavCounts badge IDs follow the pattern navCount{Feature} (e.g., navCountCrm) and are updated via updateNavCount().
i18n locale files must be full content copies for fr/zh fallbacks — re-exports (barrel files) are flagged by biome as lint/performance/noBarrelFile.
The crm feature must be declared in both moltis-web/Cargo.toml and propagated from crates/cli/Cargo.toml using moltis-web?/crm syntax.
style.css is gitignored and must be rebuilt via npx tailwindcss in crates/web/ui/ before cargo check will pass.
NavCounts uses tokio::join! for concurrent queries; adding CRM count requires adding list_contacts() to the join block and computing .len() on the result array.

code.finding: GonData struct had crm_enabled field declared but not initialized; NavCounts had crm: usize field but list_contacts() was not called in the tokio::join! block — both were pre-existing gaps on the branch.
crm feature was missing entirely — cfg!(feature = "crm") would always evaluate to false without it.
crm feature needed moltis-web?/crm added alongside existing dep:moltis-crm and moltis-gateway/crm entries.
MattersTab and InteractionsTab functions had contactId in destructuring params but read from module-level signals instead — biome flagged as unused parameters.
All 14 CRM RPC methods (crm.contacts.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.matters.*, crm.interactions.*, crm.channels.*) already registered here — confirmed zero new Rust handlers needed.

workflow: Headless autonomous sessions require all architecture decisions to be pre-resolved in workpad comments before the implementation run begins.
Running cargo check before and after changes catches feature-flag issues early — especially important when cfg!(feature) gates are involved.
biome --write catches and auto-fixes most formatting issues; barrel file warnings (noBarrelFile) require manual content duplication rather than re-exports.
Nightly rustfmt must be applied (not just checked) when formatting changes are needed, then re-verified with --check.

friction: style.css was missing from the dev environment (gitignored artifact) causing cargo check to fail until manually rebuilt with npx tailwindcss.
The pre-existing branch had incomplete struct initializations (NavCounts.crm and GonData.crm_enabled) that required investigation to diagnose why cargo check failed.
moltis-web Cargo.toml was missing the crm feature entirely — cfg!(feature = "crm") silently always false with no compile error, only caught by reasoning about feature propagation.
Waiting for async cargo check/test TaskOutput required polling workarounds — background task output retrieval was unreliable.
fr/zh locale files had to be rewritten from clean re-exports to full content copies due to biome barrel file rule, adding unnecessary boilerplate.
open_items: E2E Playwright test implementation for CRM flows (crm.spec.js) — deferred to separate ticket, requires E2E infra with CRM feature enabled.
Contact count optimization: add count_contacts() method returning SELECT COUNT(*) instead of loading all contacts in build_nav_counts (only matters at 1000+ contacts).
LIKE wildcard escaping for % and _ in CRM search terms in SqliteCrmStore::list_filtered (P3, cosmetic, single-tenant only).
Optimistic locking for concurrent contact/matter updates — last-write-wins is acceptable for v1 single-tenant but flagged as known limitation.
French and Chinese translations — fr/zh locale files currently contain English fallback strings; proper translations are a separate i18n pass.
PR creation and Linear workpad update — the implementation run ended before git push and gh pr create were confirmed complete.
